alignments.pub_claim_text: The model could be expanded to include other forms of communication or effectors for the computational design of synthetic developmental trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The suggestion to expand the model to other communication modes/effectors is a new forward-looking conclusion not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rate at which IFT motors are recruited to begin transport into the flagellum is anticorrelated with the flagellar length. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rate at which IFT motors are recruited to begin transport into the flagellum is anticorrelated with the flagellar length. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical claim verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cells may contain a mechanism for measuring flagellar length based on the communication between the base and the tip of the flagellum. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cells may contain a mechanism for measuring flagellar length based on the communication between the base and the tip of the flagellum. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about a length-measurement mechanism is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffusion time of the motors can serve as a proxy for length measurement in the flagellum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffusion time of the motors can serve as a proxy for length measurement in the flagellum. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanistic result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffusion model can achieve a stable steady-state length without the addition of any other signaling molecules or pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffusion model can achieve a stable steady-state length without the addition of any other signaling molecules or pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The model behavior and conclusion are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffusion model is able to produce the anticorrelation between length and IFT recruitment rate observed in quantitative imaging studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffusion model is able to produce the anticorrelation between length and IFT recruitment rate observed in quantitative imaging studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this empirical-model correspondence claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed stGCL model achieves superior clustering performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on spatial transcriptomics data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: stGCL reliably identifies spatial domains in tissue architecture. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: stGCL successfully reconstructs three-dimensional brain tissue structures by integrating vertical and horizontal slices. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Application of stGCL in human bronchiolar adenoma data reveals intratumor spatial heterogeneity and identifies candidate gene biomarkers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not mention the specific bronchiolar adenoma application or biomarker identification; this specific applied result is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: stGCL introduces a pioneering spatial coordinate correcting and registering strategy for tissue slices integration, which reduces batch effects and identifies cross-sectional domains precisely. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: stGCL demonstrates generalizability across platforms and resolutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Generalizability across platforms/resolutions is a new emphasis in the publication not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: stGCL employs a histology-based Vision Transformer for morphological feature extraction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The use of a histology-based Vision Transformer is a methodological detail introduced in the publication not claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stereopy introduces a multi-sample cell community detection (CCD) algorithm that can detect specific cell communities and identify genes responsible for pathological changes in comparable datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stereopy enables the investigation of specific cell communities and genes responsible for pathological changes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatially resolved temporal gene pattern inference (TGPI) algorithm enhances the identification of important genes, domains, and regulatory factors by detecting spatiotemporal gene patterns while considering spatial and temporal features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stereopy allows for the detection of spatiotemporal gene patterns by considering spatial and temporal features. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the TGPI capability but describes it with reduced certainty and a slightly more general phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NicheReg3D reconstructs 3D cell niches to enable the inference of cell-gene interaction networks within the spatial texture, bridging intercellular communications and intracellular regulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stereopy infers a three-dimensional niche-based cell-gene interaction network that bridges intercellular communications and intracellular regulations. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stereopy serves as a bioinformatics toolbox and an extensible framework that provides researchers with enhanced data interpretation abilities for mining multi-sample spatial transcriptomics data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stereopy is a flexible framework for modeling and dissecting comparative and spatiotemporal patterns in multi-sample spatial transcriptomics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Stereopy includes a universal container, a scope controller, and an integrative transformer for multi-sample multimodal data storage, management, and processing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BertNDA outperforms the state-of-the-art method in predicting ncRNA-disease associations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BertNDA outperforms state-of-the-art methods in prediction assignment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BertNDA exhibits significant potential for various biological applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BirdNET identifications can be highly reliable if a sufficiently high minimum confidence threshold is used.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current recall of the algorithm is markedly low when the minimum confidence threshold is adjusted to ensure high levels of precision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: F1-scores remain moderate (<0.5) for all datasets and confidence thresholds studied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: F1-scores for BirdNET identifications remain moderate (<0.5) across all datasets and confidence thresholds studied.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BirdNET performance is not significantly influenced by the type of recorder used or the habitat recorded but is modulated by the volume of species-specific acoustic data available online.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim addresses recorder/habitat non-effects or modulation by online species-specific data volume, indicating this specific preprint conclusion was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A judicious use of AI-based IDs provided by BirdNET can represent a novel and powerful method to assist in the assessment of bird community composition.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BirdNET-Analyzer v2.4 is a highly reliable tool for assessing bird community composition through automated analysis of ecoacoustic data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of species- and context-specific minimum confidence thresholds could significantly improve the performance of BirdNET in identifying bird species.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed PyTorch-based phosphene simulator transforms specific electrode stimulation patterns into biologically plausible representations of artificial visual percepts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modular approach of the work makes it ideal for integrating new insights in artificial vision and for hypothesis testing.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods allow researchers to design more efficient multiplex imaging panels that maximize the amount of information retained with a limited number of markers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Robust attentional guidance was observed when one of the memory colors was presented as the target or the distractor.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Robust attentional guidance was observed when one of the memory colors was presented as the target or the distractor.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulated RT distributions and error rates revealed a better match of the Multiple-Item-Template hypothesis to the observed data than the Single-Item-Template hypothesis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide behavioral and computational evidence for the concurrent guidance of attention by multiple items in visual working memory.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide behavioral and computational evidence for the concurrent guidance of attention by multiple items in visual working memory.

alignments.pub_claim_text: All three prediction algorithms make remarkably similar and high-quality predictions for one large protein, HCO_11565, from a nematode.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The glutamatergic neurons of the cuneiform nucleus are identified as a potentially clinically relevant target to improve locomotor activity in parkinsonian conditions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of deep neural networks allows for the inference of missing bioactivity signatures for any compound of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of deep neural networks allows for the inference of missing bioactivity signatures for any compound of interest. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Bioactivity signatures dynamically evolve with new data and processing strategies, unlike static chemical descriptors. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Chemical Checker is a large collection of processed, harmonized, and integrated bioactivity signatures for over 1 million small molecules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed neural network model incorporates three classes of HVC neurons based on pharmacologically identified ion channels and synaptic currents. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model reproduces the in vivo activity patterns of each class of HVC neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network unveils key intrinsic and synaptic mechanisms that govern the sequential propagation of neural activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model unveils key intrinsic and synaptic mechanisms that govern the sequential propagation of neural activity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model highlights important roles for various ionic currents and synaptic currents in the HVC network responsible for song production. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model highlights important roles for various ionic currents and synaptic currents in the HVC network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Action video gamers have enhanced functional and structural connectivity in the dorsal visual stream compared to nongamers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Action video gamers have enhanced functional and structural connectivity in the dorsal visual stream compared to nongamers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the main comparative finding unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increased functional connectivity between the left Superior Occipital Gyrus and the left Superior Parietal Lobule correlates with response time in gamers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is heightened functional connectivity between the left superior occipital gyrus and the left superior parietal lobule during a moving-dot discrimination decision-making task in action video gamers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural connectivity in the visual pathways, as measured by diffusion fractional anisotropy and quantitative anisotropy, is enhanced for gamers compared to nongamers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural connectivity in the dorsal stream is enhanced for gamers as quantified by diffusion fractional anisotropy and quantitative anisotropy measures. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These connectivity changes in the dorsal visual stream underpin the superior performance of action video gamers in vision-based decision-making tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased connectivity in the dorsal visual stream correlates with faster response times in gamers. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed the relationship as underpinning (causal/ explanatory) superior performance, whereas the publication reports a correlation with faster response times, reducing causal strength and tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide insights into how action video gaming can induce targeted neuroplastic changes in the brain. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings provide insights into how action video gaming induces improvements in connectivity between specific brain regions in visual processing pathways. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution but specifies that the insights concern connectivity improvements between particular regions, clarifying the nature of the proposed neuroplastic changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: fMRI carries rather fine-grained information about individual objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: fMRI carries rather fine-grained information about individual objects.
alignments.rationale: The claim text is identical between preprint and publication, but the stated certainty decreased (medium -> low), so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multivariate decoding and representational similarity analysis revealed high image-specific reliability in low- and high-level visual regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multivariate decoding and representational similarity analysis revealed high image-specific reliability in low- and high-level visual regions.
alignments.rationale: The result statement is the same in wording and certainty between versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representations in the lateral occipital complex contain higher category-specific information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Representations in the lateral occipital complex (LOC) appeared to also contain higher category-specific information.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding but hedges slightly ('appeared to'), which clarifies/softens the original claim without changing scope or confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Commonly employed fMRI protocols may reveal subtle yet reliable distinctions between individual objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Commonly employed fMRI protocols may reveal subtle yet reliable distinctions between individual objects.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim with the same certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Maximum Entropy method can be applied to an Integrate-and-fire model to study criticality and finite-size effects in spontaneous neuronal activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Maximum Entropy method has been applied for the first time to an Integrate-and-fire model that can be tuned at criticality.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same methodological contribution but adds emphasis ('for the first time') and specifies tuning at criticality, clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generalized Ising models accurately predict the average local activities and correlation functions between neurons in the critical state of the Integrate-and-fire model networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized Ising Hamiltonians exhibit a spin glass phase for low temperatures, characterized by mostly negative intrinsic fields and a bimodal distribution of interaction constants.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes statistical-mechanical properties (spin glass phase) rather than the original predictive claim about local activities and correlations, indicating a shift in focus and a narrowing of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks with a higher percentage of inhibitory neurons lead to Ising-like systems with reduced thermal fluctuations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks with a higher percentage of inhibitory neurons lead to Ising-like systems with reduced thermal fluctuations.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried over verbatim with the same stated certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subsampling affects the efficiency of the mapping of neuronal data to Ising-like models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Considering only neuronal pairs associated with the largest correlation functions allows the study of larger system sizes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the subsampling issue as a specific practical approach (selecting largest correlations) to study larger systems, clarifying the original limitation rather than contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The three-point correlation functions across triplets of neurons can be predicted qualitatively by generalized Ising models.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize the three-point correlation prediction present in the preprint, indicating this claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A bio-inspired network motif can explicitly address the issue of context-dependent computations in artificial networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A bio-inspired network motif can explicitly address the challenge of flexibility in artificial systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same contribution using slightly broader language ('flexibility'), clarifying the framing without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture with additional 'switching' units can learn new contexts while maintaining performance in previous contexts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bottleneck-switching network enables learning of new contexts without catastrophic forgetting through inhibition of redundant background features.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result (learning new contexts without forgetting) while adding a proposed mechanism (inhibition of redundant features), which clarifies the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The switching network outperforms networks without switching when the task is difficult due to high transparency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bottleneck-switching network enables learning of new contexts without catastrophic forgetting through inhibition of redundant background features.
alignments.rationale: The publication's result about the bottleneck-switching network preventing forgetting implies better performance versus non-switching networks under challenging conditions; the published text adds mechanistic detail and reframes the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elastic weight consolidation is not successful in the context of the proposed architecture, while progressive nets are more complex but less effective.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific comparative conclusion about EWC and progressive nets present in the preprint, suggesting this claim was dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Poultry type acted as a barrier to the transmission of the virus among midwestern poultry farms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Poultry type acted as a barrier to the transmission of the HPAI virus among midwestern poultry farms.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in both versions, with consistent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A continuous external source of virus was not needed to explain the propagation of HPAI cases within the commercial poultry industries after the initial introduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The propagation of HPAI cases was self-sustainable within the commercial poultry industries after initial introduction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion using equivalent wording (self-sustainable), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Within state viral transitions occurred more frequently than inter-state transitions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Within state viral transitions occurred more frequently than inter-state transitions.
alignments.rationale: The result is repeated verbatim with similar certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distance, road density, and proportion of water coverage were associated with viral transition between county groups.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distance and sample size were very strongly supported as associated with viral transition between county groups.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the set of supported predictors (removing road density and water coverage, adding sample size), which revises and narrows the original set of associations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human-mediated viral transportation played a key role in the spread of the highly pathogenic H5N2 outbreak in North America.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The outbreak was shaped by poultry industries and geographic factors, indicating that different types of midwestern poultry industries were not a single homogenous population.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a more nuanced interpretation emphasizing industry and geographic structure rather than explicitly attributing a key role to human-mediated transport, so the claim is revised in emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Overall tumour growth is most extensive when both cell types can stably coexist.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Overall tumour growth is most extensive when both cell types can stably coexist.
alignments.rationale: The result is identical between preprint and publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When inter-species competition exceeds intra-species competition, the populations spatially separate and invasion arrests.
alignments.pub_claim_text: When inter-species competition exceeds intra-species competition, the populations spatially separate and invasion arrests.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim and unchanged in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spatial and ecological relationship between a heterogeneous population of tumour cells is a key factor in determining their ability to cooperate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial and ecological relationship between a heterogeneous population of tumour cells is a key factor in determining their ability to cooperate.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same in both versions, with no substantive differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tumours in which different phenotypes coexist stably are more invasive than tumours in which phenotypes are spatially separated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tumours in which different phenotypes coexist stably are more invasive than tumours in which phenotypes are spatially separated.
alignments.rationale: The statement is unchanged in content and certainty; the publication labels it as a prediction but the textual claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bagging-based ensemble learning technique with a support vector machine base model achieved the best classification results for ADHD diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bagging-based ensemble learning technique with a support vector machine base model achieved the best classification results for ADHD diagnosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about the bagging-based SVM ensemble achieving the best classification results; no substantive change in wording, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nodal efficiency in the right inferior frontal gyrus and right middle frontal-inferior parietal functional connectivity significantly contributed to the accurate discrimination between ADHD probands and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Features of nodal efficiency in the right inferior frontal gyrus and right middle frontal-inferior parietal functional connectivity significantly contributed to accurate discrimination between ADHD probands and controls. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same features but with slightly different wording and a lower reported certainty (high -> medium), so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher nodal efficiency of the right middle frontal gyrus is linked to symptom remission in ADHD, while higher functional connectivity between the right middle frontal gyrus and inferior parietal lobe is associated with symptom persistence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher nodal efficiency of the right middle frontal gyrus is linked to symptom remission in ADHD, while higher right middle frontal-inferior parietal functional connectivity is associated with symptom persistence. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same association between nodal efficiency/connectivity and remission/persistence with similar certainty and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ensemble learning techniques may have the potential to identify more reliable neurobiological markers for severe brain disorders due to their improved robustness over basic classifiers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Findings suggest that ensemble learning techniques may have the potential to identify more reliable neurobiological markers for neurodevelopmental disorders. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the target domain from 'severe brain disorders' to 'neurodevelopmental disorders' and lowers certainty (medium -> low), representing a substantive revision and tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the receiver operating characteristic curve was 0.89 for ADHD versus controls and 0.9 for ADHD persisters versus remitters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds numerical performance metrics (AUC values) that were not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A universal expression-based diagnostic biomarker panel for HCC was identified using large scale transcriptomic profiling datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A panel of three genes (FCN3, CLEC1B, and PRC1) was identified as a biomarker for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) with an accuracy between 93 and 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the gene identities and performance metrics for the biomarker panel described in the preprint, which clarifies and concretizes the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The three-genes panel-based HCC biomarker classified HCC samples and non-tumorous samples with an accuracy between 93 to 98% and AUROC in a range of 0.97 to 1.0. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Area Under Receiver Operating Characteristic curve (AUROC) for the three-genes-based HCC biomarker ranged from 0.97 to 1.0 in training/validation datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same AUROC range for the three-gene panel as stated in the preprint; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The identified genes are independent prognostic indicators for various types of survivals in HCC patients and significantly stratify high-risk and low-risk patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The identified genes serve as prognostic indicators for various types of survival in HCC patients, significantly stratifying high-risk and low-risk patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the prognostic utility of the identified genes with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study led to the development of a webserver HCCPred to provide service to the scientific community. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention the webserver described in the preprint, so this contribution appears omitted from the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A reasonable performance (AUROC 0.91-0.96) was achieved on a validation dataset containing peripheral blood mononuclear cells, indicating the non-invasive utility of the biomarker. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new validation result on PBMCs and highlights non-invasive utility, which was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feeding regime of early supplementary solid diet improved rumen development and ruminant production. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feeding regime of an early supplementary solid diet improved rumen development and production in goat kids. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the subject (goat kids) rather than the broader 'ruminant production' phrasing in the preprint, narrowing the claim's scope while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The volatile fatty acids fermented by microbes increased significantly in the supplementary solid diet groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Volatile fatty acids, including acetate, propionate, and butyrate, significantly increased in the supplementary solid diet groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which volatile fatty acids increased (acetate, propionate, butyrate), clarifying the preprint's general statement without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of intersecting core species contributed to rumen function shifts by solid diet. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predominant genera in the rumen microbiome altered significantly from unclassified Sphingobacteriaceae in the non-supplementary group to Prevotella in the supplementary solid diet groups. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides specific genus-level shifts underlying the preprint's general statement about core species; this is a clarification with stronger reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Core microbiome manipulation by feeding strategies can increase animal products. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reproduce the forward-looking causal recommendation that microbiome manipulation can increase animal products; that prescriptive claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified core community structures that were affected by diet and associated with rumen development and goat production. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides mechanisms of the microbiome governed by a solid diet regime early in life and highlights the signature microbiota involved in animal health and production. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the identified community structures as mechanisms and highlights signature microbiota tied to health/production; wording and certainty differ slightly but the underlying finding is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMNN provides improved merging within the corresponding cell types across batches, leading to reduced differentiation across batches compared to MNN, Seurat v3, and LIGER. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMNN provides improved merging within the corresponding cell types across batches compared to MNN, Seurat v3, and LIGER. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core claim that SMNN improves merging across cell types relative to other methods with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMNN retains more cell type-specific features, with differentially expressed genes identified between cell types after SMNN correction being biologically more relevant. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMNN retains more cell-type-specific features, with differentially expressed genes identified after SMNN correction being biologically more relevant. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about SMNN preserving cell-type-specific features and biologically relevant DE genes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The precision of identifying differentially expressed genes improves by up to 841% after applying SMNN for batch effect correction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The precision of differentially expressed genes improves by up to 841.0% after SMNN correction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same quantitative improvement in DE gene precision (841%) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing batch effect correction methods that ignore cell type information may lead to undesired correction results, particularly when batch effects are significant compared to biological effects. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this limitation about other methods; the cautionary statement appears omitted from the published summary.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SMNN leads to reduced differentiation across batches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states reduced differentiation across batches as an explicit claim; although implicit in the preprint, it appears as a separate explicit publication claim not directly matched one-to-one with a single preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Profile-QSAR (pQSAR) demonstrates a median correlation of R 2 ext =0.53, which is significantly higher than the median correlation of R 2 ext =0.05 for the 11,805 single-assay RFR models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pQSAR method demonstrates a median correlation of prediction with experimental results of r_ext² = 0.53, which is comparable to four-concentration experimental IC₅₀s. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 0.53 median correlation result but frames it relative to experimental IC50s rather than explicitly contrasting with the RFR 0.05 figure; the core metric is retained but contextualized differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 72% of the pQSAR models achieved the standard success threshold of R 2 ext >0.30, totaling 8558 successful models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 72% of the pQSAR models achieved the standard success threshold of r_ext² > 0.30, totaling 8558 successful models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same success-rate statistic for pQSAR models without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: pQSAR can be applied to virtually any disease area, as it includes assays from all of the 51 annotated target sub-classes and 4196 phenotypic assays. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pQSAR method can be applied to virtually any disease area, as it includes assays from all 51 annotated target subclasses and 4196 phenotypic assays. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same broad applicability claim with essentially identical details and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pQSAR method is updated monthly to include new measurements and can make predictions for 5.5 million Novartis compounds, totaling 50 billion predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pQSAR method is updated monthly to include new measurements and can make predictions for 5.5 million NVS compounds, totaling 50 billion predictions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim about monthly updates and the 5.5 million / 50 billion prediction capability but uses slightly different naming (NVS) and reports a lower certainty (high -> medium).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The random forest regression models showed a median correlation of prediction with the experiment of only r_ext² = 0.05, indicating virtually random predictions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports the poor performance (r_ext² = 0.05) of single-assay random forest models as a separate claim; although related to the preprint's comparative statement, it appears as an explicit publication-only result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Broad-range pathogen surveillance using metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) provides an unbiased assessment for possible infectious etiologies in pediatric meningitis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Case-control CSF mNGS surveys can complement conventional diagnostic methods to identify etiologies of meningitis, conduct surveillance, and predict outbreaks.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's general claim about unbiased mNGS assessment as a role in complementing diagnostics and enabling surveillance/prediction, broadening the stated applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In idiopathic cases of meningitis, mNGS identified a potential etiology in 40% of cases, including bacterial and viral pathogens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In idiopathic cases, mNGS identified a potential bacterial or viral etiology in 40%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative result and etiologic categories with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study revealed 17 additional cases of CHIKV meningitis through qPCR of stored CSF samples from children who presented with idiopathic meningitis in 2017.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CHIKV-specific qPCR of all remaining stored CSF samples from children who presented with idiopathic meningitis in 2017 revealed 17 additional CHIKV meningitis cases, exposing an unrecognized meningitis outbreak.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the 17-case finding but adds the interpretation that this exposure uncovered an unrecognized outbreak, amplifying epidemiological significance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was 83% concordance between mNGS and conventional testing in samples with known infectious causes of meningitis and without infections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In samples with known etiology and without infections, there was 83% concordance between mNGS and conventional testing.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the concordance percentage and context verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Case-control CSF mNGS surveys can complement conventional diagnostic methods to identify etiologies of meningitis and facilitate informed policy decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved patient- and population-level data can inform evidence-based policy decisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the policy-use interpretation into a statement about improved data informing evidence-based policy, clarifying rather than contradicting the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Including population-level parametric lesion probabilities with respect to age and using alternative machine learning techniques provided negligible improvement in WMH segmentation performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Including population-level parametric lesion probabilities with respect to age and using alternative machine learning techniques provided negligible improvement in WMH segmentation performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this negative result about those model augmentations with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCally Adaptive Threshold Estimation (LOCATE) provided a substantial improvement in the lesion segmentation performance compared to the global thresholding currently used in BIANCA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCally Adaptive Threshold Estimation (LOCATE) provided a substantial improvement in lesion segmentation performance compared to global thresholding.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the core performance improvement claim for LOCATE without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LOCATE adapts well to wide variations in lesion load and spatial distribution as validated on a cohort of CADASIL patients and healthy controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCATE adapts well to wide variations in lesion load and spatial distribution as validated on a cohort of CADASIL patients and healthy controls.
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: LOCATE allowed for the detection of more deep lesions and better segmentation of periventricular lesion boundaries despite differences in lesion spatial distribution and load across datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a more specific performance advantage (deep lesion detection and boundary segmentation) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An efficient machine learning approach was developed to fit parameters of a biological model related to the binding of the shrimp protein Pen a 1 with antibody-receptor complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parameters for the BioNetGen model were optimized using an adaptive-network-based fuzzy inference system.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the exact adaptive fuzzy-inference method used to optimize model parameters, clarifying the preprint's description of an ML approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fuzzy system that uses a residual-sum-of-squares value as the input value performs acceptably well for optimizing the BioNetGen model parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fuzzy system that uses a residual-sum-of-squares value as the input value performs acceptably well.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same performance assessment for the residual-sum-of-squares-based fuzzy system.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of fuzzy systems that use probabilities as their input values is inconsistent and requires further development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the fuzzy systems that use probabilities as their input values performed inconsistently and need further development.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same limitation statement about probability-input fuzzy systems needing further development.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further research could include similar studies using other training or clustering algorithms to improve the methodology for fitting biological models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This methodology could potentially be modified for use in fitting other biological models.
alignments.rationale: The preprint suggests methodological refinements (different algorithms) while the publication reframes future work as applying the methodology to other biological models, shifting focus and broadening the suggested direction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiTaxa identified 17 out of 29 taxa with confirmed links to periodontitis, achieving a recall of 0.59, compared to 3 out of 29 taxa with a recall of 0.10 by the state-of-the-art method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiTaxa outperformed the OTU-based state-of-the-art approach in finding biomarkers in both resolution and coverage.
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the preprint's specific biomarker-detection numbers into a general claim of outperforming OTU-based methods in resolution and coverage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On synthetic benchmark data, DiTaxa obtained full precision and recall in biomarker detection, while the state-of-the-art method achieved 0.91 and 0.90, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiTaxa outperformed the OTU-based state-of-the-art approach in finding biomarkers in both resolution and coverage.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a high-level statement of superior biomarker detection that likely subsumes the synthetic-benchmark precision/recall result but omits the explicit synthetic-data metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine-learning classifiers trained to predict host disease phenotypes based on the NPE representation performed competitively to the state-of-the-art using OTUs or k-mers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiTaxa performed competitively to the k-mer based state-of-the-art approach in phenotype prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the preprint's claim (competitive with OTUs or k-mers) to specify competitiveness with the k-mer based approach only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiTaxa substantially outperformed OTU features for the rheumatoid arthritis dataset with a macro-F1 score of 0.76 compared to 0.65.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiTaxa outperformed the OTU-based state-of-the-art approach in finding biomarkers in both resolution and coverage.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the RA dataset performance as part of a general superiority in biomarker finding, omitting the specific F1 scores but aligning in conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiTaxa can efficiently run on large datasets, completing the analysis of a 16S rRNA dataset of 1359 samples in approximately 1.5 hours on 20 cores.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the runtime/efficiency claim; this specific computational performance statement is absent from the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DiTaxa is a new paradigm for micro-biome phenotype and biomarker detection that uses alignment- and reference-free subsequence based 16S rRNA data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the method as a 'new paradigm,' a higher-level characterization that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A substantial proportion of the school-age population experience cognitive-related learning difficulties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A substantial proportion of the school-age population experience cognitive-related learning difficulties.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the prevalence statement directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The project applies a transdiagnostic approach to discover the underpinning mechanisms of learning problems in children.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The project applies a transdiagnostic approach to discover the underpinning mechanisms of learning problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the transdiagnostic project aim with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study utilizes hypothesis-free data-driven methods to identify the cognitive, behavioural and neural dimensions of learning difficulties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-systems approach enhances understanding of the common and complex needs of children who struggle at school.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's methodological description into a conclusion about how the multi-systems approach enhances understanding, clarifying outcomes rather than restating the exact methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The broad recruitment criteria targeting all children with cognitive learning problems, irrespective of diagnoses and comorbidities, make the sample unique.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The broad recruitment criteria targeting all children with cognitive learning problems makes the sample unique.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim about broad recruitment criteria making the sample unique.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset will provide a valuable resource of genetic, imaging and cognitive developmental data for the scientific community.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset will provide a valuable resource of genetic, imaging and cognitive developmental data for the scientific community.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the dataset resource claim but with higher stated certainty, effectively amplifying the confidence in its value.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework enables reproducible and objective classification experiments in Alzheimer's disease using three publicly available datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework enables reproducible and objective classification experiments in Alzheimer's disease using three publicly available datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so there is no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FDG PET outperformed T1 MRI for all classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FDG PET outperformed T1 MRI for all classification tasks in the evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but adds "in the evaluation," a minor clarification about the context of the claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Linear SVM and L2-logistic regression resulted in similar performance and both outperformed random forests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linear SVM and L2-logistic regression resulted in similar performance and both outperformed random forests. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced exactly in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: No difference in performance was found for the use of different atlases, image smoothing, partial volume correction of FDG PET images, or feature type. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Measures to reduce risk of bias should be employed to increase the internal validity of studies on CIPN. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Power calculations and other factors, such as clinical relevance, should inform the choice of outcome measure in study design for CIPN. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using both sexes of animals in the modelling of CIPN will likely improve external validity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The recommendation to use both sexes to improve external validity is present in the publication but was not an explicit preprint claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Follow-up studies will validate the predictions of the virtual models with tDCS-magnetoencephalography in a clinical AD patient cohort. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The text and intent are the same though the publication labels the item as a contribution rather than a conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local achromatic cues like contrast, penumbral blur, and texture play an important role in distinguishing edges caused by shadows from those caused by surface boundaries. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Logistic regression on a Gabor Filter bank separates the categories with nearly 80% accuracy and exhibits tuning to penumbral blur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The broad preprint claim about multiple achromatic cues is presented in the publication as more specific model evidence (penumbral blur/tuning), narrowing the generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both the highest contrast as well as the lowest contrast edges are more likely to be occlusions than shadows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both the highest contrast as well as the lowest contrast edges are more likely to be occlusions than shadows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about contrast extremes in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contrast cues alone can reliably distinguish the two edge categories with nearly 70% accuracy at 40×40 resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contrast cues alone can reliably distinguish the two edge categories with nearly 70% accuracy at 40x40 resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative result retained in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Filter-Rectify Filter style neural network extending the Gabor Filter bank model performed at better than 80% accuracy and exhibited greater sensitivity to texture differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Filter-Rectify Filter style neural network performed at better than 80% accuracy and exhibited greater sensitivity to texture differences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The FRF model performance and texture sensitivity claim is preserved exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FRF model shows better agreement with human performance in classifying edges as shadows or occlusion boundaries than the GFB model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best human observers attain the same performance as the machine classifiers in edge classification tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized model-vs-model agreement with humans, whereas the publication emphasizes parity between top human observers and classifiers; the focus and comparison changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Deep Global Balancing Cox regression (DGBCox) method demonstrates robust and stable performance for breast cancer prognosis under data distribution shift. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel method Deep Global Balancing Cox regression (DGBCox) provides stable breast cancer prognosis in situations with data distribution shifts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DGBCox outperforms benchmark methods in terms of both prediction accuracy and stability when applied to 12 test datasets from different breast cancer studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DGBCox outperforms benchmark methods in terms of both prediction accuracy and stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Core performance and comparative advantage are retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top 50 ranked genes identified by the DGBCox model suggest that the cell cycle and organelle organization are the most relevant biological processes for stable breast cancer prognosis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the gene-ranking / biological-process claim present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assumption that test and training data follow the same distribution is often violated in breast cancer prognosis due to the disease's heterogeneity. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods may not provide stable predictions when data distribution shifts occur. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for breast cancer prognosis can potentially be adapted for application to other cancer types. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the stated potential generalization to other cancer types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Natural selection is capable of discovering adaptive forms of cell plasticity associated with arbitrarily complex logical functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Natural selection is capable of discovering adaptive forms of cell plasticity associated with complex logical functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the word 'arbitrarily,' slightly tempering the universality claim while preserving the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Developmental dynamics causes simpler functions to evolve more readily than complex ones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Developmental dynamics cause simpler functions to evolve more readily than complex ones. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement is equivalent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolution of plasticity enables cells to display appropriate plastic responses to environmental conditions that they have not experienced in their evolutionary past. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolution of plasticity enables cells to display appropriate plastic responses to environmental conditions that they have not experienced in their evolutionary past. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are non-trivial functional parallelisms between learning in neural networks and the action of natural selection on environmentally sensitive gene regulatory networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developmental bias can be interpreted as a learning bias in the acquisition of plasticity functions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the parallelism as an interpretable learning bias, clarifying the conceptual link between learning and selection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed theoretical framework offers insights into the evolution of plastic responses that integrate information from multiple cues, which underpins the evolution of multicellularity and developmental robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work offers a theoretical framework for the evolution of plastic responses that integrate information from multiple cues. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Weighted 𝓁 1 -norm Non-Parallel Support Vector Machine (𝓁 1 -WNPSVM) achieves a 97% balanced accuracy in predicting disease status. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The $$\ell _1$$-WNPSVM method outperforms traditional machine learning techniques, achieving a $$97\%$$ balanced accuracy on imbalanced data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies a set of 253 genes exclusively expressed in the lungs and spleen tissues of infected mice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We discovered sets of 235 genes exclusively expressed in the lungs and spleen tissues, which can classify bacterial strains and controls. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gene ontology analysis reveals that the immune system pathway is activated in both lungs and spleen tissues. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gene expression (transcription), immune system, and disease (cancer) pathways are activated in the spleen. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodologies integrate transcriptomic datasets from the lungs and spleen tissues infected by Francisella tularensis — Schu4 and Live Vaccine Strain (LVS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of genomic datasets can aid in the development of vaccines, diagnostics, and targeted therapeutics against difficult-to-treat pathogens and cancer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses the computational impracticality of applying existing NPSVM-type methods to gene expression datasets with a high number of genes relative to samples. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis shows that signal transduction and disease (cancer) pathways are the most significant pathways activated in the lungs. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spherical Texture extraction provides a 20-value characterization that quantifies the scale of features in the spherical projection of the intensity distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spherical Texture extraction provides a 20-value characterization that quantifies the scale of features in the spherical projection of the intensity distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Spherical Texture extraction characterizes biologically defined gene expression patterns in Drosophila melanogaster embryos, giving a quantitative read-out for pattern formation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification of germline nuclei using their Spherical Texture outperforms a convolutional neural net when training data is limited. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Spherical Texture method is implemented as a plugin in ilastik, making it easy to install and apply to any segmented 3D or 2D dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cell responsivity within a soft, fiber-reinforced fibrin gel microenvironment was influenced by the spatial localization of individual cells around a stiffer polyglycolide-co-caprolactone fiber. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis accounts for the speed of learning in previous experiments with incompatible and compatible virtual surgery tasks, and with visuomotor rotation tasks with different numbers of targets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results provide theoretical principles for the design of motor tasks that allow for faster learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide theoretical principles for the design of motor tasks that allow for faster learning. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mono-ubiquitylated linker histones (HUb) are expected to influence DNA compaction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: OptiFit provides a suitable option for users requiring consistent OTU assignments at the same quality as afforded by de novo clustering methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The drug combination sensitivity score (CSS) is highly reproducible among replicates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CSS-based synergy score is able to detect true synergistic and antagonistic drug combinations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S synergy score can detect true synergistic and antagonistic drug combinations with an accuracy level comparable to that of the full matrix design. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cross drug combination design coupled with the CSS scoring facilitates the evaluation of drug combination sensitivity and synergy using the same scale. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cross design enables a more cost-effective and simultaneous testing of drug combination sensitivity and synergy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed computational techniques are expected to be easily applicable and beneficial in the field of drug combination discovery. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental-computational approach could improve the discovery rate in high-throughput drug combination screening, especially for primary patient samples. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selective sweeps occur even for traits under relatively weak selection and where the genetic background explains most of the variation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selective sweeps occur even for traits under relatively weak selection and where the genetic background explains most of the variation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New mutations can be important for traits under strong stabilizing selection that undergo a large optimum shift. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New mutations can be important for traits under strong stabilizing selection that undergo a large optimum shift. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Population bottlenecks and expansion impact overall genetic variation as well as the relative importance of sweeps from standing variation and the speed with which adaptation can occur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Population bottlenecks and expansion impact overall genetic variation as well as the relative importance of sweeps from standing variation and the speed with which adaptation can occur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result verbatim and with the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our simulations qualitatively recapitulate differences between two traits under selection during maize domestication. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulations qualitatively recapitulate differences between traits under selection during maize domestication. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Demography and the effect sizes of mutations have the largest influence on genetic variation after adaptation. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nineteen features appeared to be significantly correlated with phase III clinical trial outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nineteen features appeared to be significantly correlated with phase III clinical trial outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Only 4 features passed validation schemes that assessed feature robustness and generalizability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Only 4 features passed validation schemes that assessed feature robustness and generalizability. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful targets tended to have lower mean mRNA expression and higher expression variance than failed targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful targets tended to have lower mean expression and higher expression variance than failed targets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is favorable for a target to be present in the tissue(s) affected by a disease and absent from other tissues. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work demonstrates the feasibility of predicting clinical trial outcomes from target features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Classifiers with a single feature performed as well in cross-validation as classifiers with more features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The construction of perturbative maps from genome-scale datasets can uncover new biological associations and facilitate downstream discovery tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These maps capture known biological relationships and uncover new associations that can be used for downstream discovery tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A standardized terminology and benchmark procedures for evaluating the quality of perturbative maps have been established. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for building and benchmarking perturbative maps can enhance comparisons within and between different technologies and methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utility of the constructed maps has been demonstrated by suggesting roles for two largely uncharacterized genes. (certainty=high, type=result)
