alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in wording and meaning to the preprint result, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fitting the agents’ behavior by the model resulted in smaller weights of model-based control for the opponent SR+IR agent than for the SR-only agent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fitting the agents' behavior by the model resulted in smaller weights of model-based control for the opponent SR+IR agent than for the SR-only agent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is carried forward verbatim into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reconciles previous suggestions about OCD, indicating that impaired model-based control and memory trace imbalance may underlie the disorder. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD) is associated with impairment of model-based behavioral control. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model-based control impairment aspect of the preprint's broader reconciliation claim, narrowing the focus to one mechanism rather than the full memory-trace/multi-hypothesis framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model cannot explain the behavior of OCD patients in punishment contexts, suggesting a limitation in its applicability. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model cannot explain the behavior of OCD patients in punishment contexts, suggesting a limitation in its applicability. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Opponent learning of goal-directed and habitual systems from positive versus negative prediction errors could lead to an overall decrease in goal-directed control and enhance the obsession-compulsion cycle. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Opponent learning in model-based and model-free controllers may underlie the development of obsession-compulsion in OCD. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanistic idea in more general terms (model-based/model-free wording) while preserving the core conclusion about opponent learning driving obsession-compulsion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA-DRB1*04:01 is associated with diseases such as sclerosis, arthritis, diabetes, and Covid19. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLA-DRB1*04:01 is associated with numerous diseases, including sclerosis, arthritis, diabetes, and COVID-19. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the association claim with equivalent content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method HLA-DR4Pred2 was developed on a large dataset containing 12676 binders and an equal number of non-binders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLA-DR4Pred2 was developed on a large dataset containing 12,676 binders and an equal number of non-binders. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The development/dataset claim is carried forward unchanged (numerical formatting only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the composition-based model increased from an AUC of 0.90 to 0.93 when combined with BLAST search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the composition-based model increased to 0.93 when combined with BLAST search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance improvement claim is reproduced in the publication with effectively the same numeric result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA-DR4Pred2 achieved a maximum AUC of 0.99 on an alternate dataset containing 12676 binders and 86300 non-binders. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models developed on the realistic dataset containing 12,676 binders and 86,300 non-binders achieved a maximum AUROC of 0.99. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is preserved, with equivalent dataset description and metric.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA-DR4Pred2 performs better than existing methods when comparing the performance of the best model on the validation dataset. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed method outperformed existing methods when we compared the performance of our best model to that of existing methods on the independent dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance conclusion is maintained in the publication, phrased equivalently as outperforming existing methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach effectively summarizes spatial relationships in the tumor microenvironment (TME) using spatial K functions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach effectively identifies important spatial interactions in tumor microenvironments while controlling the false discovery rate. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's description to emphasize identification of interactions and explicitly adds the statistical control (FDR), clarifying the methodological guarantees.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates the ability to predict outcomes of interest and identify important spatial relationships in TMEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can predict outcomes of interest related to patient survival and treatment success with immunotherapies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the kinds of outcomes (survival and immunotherapy success), narrowing the general 'outcomes of interest' claim and reports a lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A custom approach based on permutation and cross-validation has been developed to evaluate the statistical significance of findings related to spatial interactions in the TME. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods proposed for analyzing tumor microenvironments are publicly available in a companion R package called funkycells. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes software release and availability of the methods described in the preprint; the core methodological contribution maps to this availability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has been validated through simulation experiments and real data sets from triple negative breast cancer and lung cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach was validated using simulation experiments and real data sets from triple negative breast cancer and lung cancer patients. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation claim is preserved unchanged between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATOMDANCE is a suite of statistical and kernel-based machine learning tools designed for comparing and denoising functional states of protein motion captured in time-series from molecular dynamics simulations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATOMDANCE is a user-interfaced suite of comparative machine learning-based denoising tools designed for identifying functional sites and the patterns of coordinated motion within molecular dynamics simulations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution while adding that the suite is user-interfaced and emphasizing identification of functional sites and coordinated motion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATOMDANCE derives empirical p-values identifying functional similarity/difference in dynamics at each amino acid site on the protein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATOMDANCE-maxDemon4.0 employs Gaussian kernel functions to compute site-wise maximum mean discrepancy, assessing denoised differences in nonrandom motions between functional or evolutionary states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the statistical approach (Gaussian kernels and site-wise MMD) behind the site-wise comparison; this clarifies the preprint's empirical p-value description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATOMDANCE allows for a site-wise test of neutral vs. non-neutral evolution in the divergence of dynamic function in protein homologs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATOMDANCE-maxDemon4.0 employs Gaussian kernel functions to compute site-wise maximum mean discrepancy, assessing denoised differences in nonrandom motions between functional or evolutionary states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames assessment between functional or evolutionary states using site-wise MMD; this is a methodological reframing of the preprint's evolutionary-test claim rather than an identical restatement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATOMDANCE demonstrates utility in identifying key sites involved in dynamic responses during functional binding interactions involving DNA, small molecule drugs, and virus-host recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparative molecular dynamics analysis facilitated by ATOMDANCE can reveal functional sites involved in dynamic responses during binding interactions and allosteric activation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core utility claim about identifying functional sites involved in binding-related dynamic responses, with modest wording shifts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ATOMDANCE can assess the effect of genetic mutation on protein motion and identifies regions of sites on proteins that tend to move together or 'resonate' as a whole unit or community. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ATOMDANCE-Choreograph2.0 employs mixed-model analysis of variance and graph network to detect regions where time-synchronized shifts in dynamics occur. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the analysis modules and methods (mixed-model ANOVA and graph network) that realize the preprint's claim about detecting regions moving together and assessing mutation effects.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons display non-linear behaviours indicative of integration processes in early stages of visual processing when probed with complex stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons in primary visual cortex display complex and non-linear response characteristics when probed with complex stimuli that extend beyond their classical receptive field. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint observation while adding explicit wording about responses extending beyond the classical receptive field, clarifying spatial extent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed extended generative model for visual scenes includes spatial dependencies among different features and predicts a connectivity structure linking neurons with similar orientation preferences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model predicts a connectivity structure linking neurons with similar orientation preferences that matches typical patterns found for long-ranging horizontal axons and feedback projections in visual cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the connectivity prediction by relating it to known anatomical patterns, which is a clarification/strengthening of the preprint's connectivity claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model replicates several hallmark effects of contextual processing and predicts characteristic differences for surround modulation between two model populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model replicates several hallmark effects of contextual processing and predicts characteristic differences for surround modulation between the two model populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A satisfactory explanation of contextual phenomena in terms of realistic interactions and dynamics in visual cortex is still missing. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model provides a novel framework for contextual processing in the visual system, proposing a well-defined functional role for horizontal axons and feedback projections. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the preprint's stated limitation with a positive claim that the model offers a novel explanatory framework, representing a substantive shift in claim tone and strength.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed extended generative model for visual scenes includes spatial dependencies among different features and derives a neurophysiologically realistic inference scheme. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly highlights the derivation of a neurophysiologically realistic inference scheme, which was not clearly stated as a separate claim in the preprint and thus appears as an added emphasis in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interaction between Rac, Rho, and phosphorylated paxillin is sufficient to generate wave-pinning that underlies cellular polarization in CHO-K1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interaction between Rac and Rho, along with phosphorylated paxillin, is sufficient to generate wave-pinning that underlies cellular polarization in CHO-K1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and content are effectively identical, asserting the same sufficiency result for wave-pinning in CHO-K1 cells.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 6D reaction-diffusion model can be reduced to a 4D spatiotemporal model that produces wave-pinning, mixed-mode oscillations (MMOs), and relaxation oscillations (ROs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reduced 4V spatiotemporal model produced mixed-mode oscillations (MMOs) and relaxation oscillations (ROs) in addition to wave-pinning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same reduction and dynamics but uses slightly different notation (4V) and emphasizes the produced behaviors explicitly, clarifying the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutating paxillin’s serine 273 residue alters the dynamics of the system from MMOs to ROs or nonoscillatory behavior through changes in the concentration of active p21-Activated Kinase 1 (PAK). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutating paxillin’s serine 273 residue alters the dynamics of the system from MMOs to ROs or nonoscillatory behavior through variation in the scaled concentration of p21-Activated Kinase 1 (PAK). (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same mechanistic effect but specifies that the change is in the scaled concentration of PAK, a minor clarification of the mechanistic detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's implementation using the Cellular Potts Model (CPM) resulted in membrane oscillations and fast directionality variations similar to those observed in CHO-K1 cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model outcomes demonstrated that protrusions in the cell membrane changed Rac–Rho localization, resulting in membrane oscillations and fast directionality variations similar to those observed experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the observed membrane oscillations and directionality dynamics but adds mechanistic detail linking protrusions to Rac–Rho localization, clarifying the model outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Perceptual noise during evidence accumulation was best described by a non-linear model of noise scaling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Perceptual judgments were well described by an accumulation process in which noise scaled nonlinearly with evidence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result using equivalent phrasing about nonlinear scaling of noise in an accumulation process.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Participants trained using feedback alone learned the game rules rapidly and used similar strategies to those who received explicit instructions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants trained with feedback alone learned the game rules rapidly and used strategies and displayed biases similar to those who received explicit instructions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates rapid learning and similar strategies and explicitly adds that participants displayed similar biases, a clarification/extension of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decisions in both groups were influenced in similar ways by biases and perceptual noise, suggesting that explicit instructions did not reduce bias or noise in pulse-based accumulation tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants trained with feedback alone learned the game rules rapidly and used strategies and displayed biases similar to those who received explicit instructions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the similarity in biases as a high-certainty observed result but does not explicitly restate the preprint's interpretive claim that instructions did not reduce noise; the empirical claim about bias is asserted with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online games inspired by evidence accumulation tasks provide a valuable large-scale behavioral assessment platform to examine perceptual decision making and learning in humans. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results challenge the conventional description of the accumulation process and suggest that online games provide a valuable platform to examine perceptual decision making and learning in humans. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the platform claim but frames results as challenging conventional descriptions of accumulation, broadening the interpretive context of the contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The feedback-based training pipeline developed for this game may be useful for evaluating perceptual decision making in human populations with difficulty following verbal instructions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This practical application of the feedback-based pipeline appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint; it is a new, added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both adaptor binding and dimensional reduction on the 2D surface are necessary to reproduce the cooperative kinetics of clathrin assembly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clathrin structures can both form and disassemble spontaneously when adaptor protein availability is low, despite high abundance of clathrin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes spontaneous formation/disassembly under low adaptor availability, which is a specific consequence related to the preprint's mechanistic requirement; wording shifts make this a clarification with moderate alignment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stable nucleation requires a stoichiometry of adaptor to clathrin that exceeds 1:1. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nucleation requires a stoichiometry of adaptor to clathrin that exceeds 1:1, meaning additional adaptor types are necessary to form lattices successfully and efficiently. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same stoichiometric requirement and expands briefly on its implication for adaptor types, but the core result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model predicts how adaptor density controls stabilization of clathrin-coated structures against spontaneous disassembly. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model predicts how adaptor density can tune clathrin-coated structures from the transient to the stable, indicating that active energy consumption is not required for lattice disassembly or remodeling during growth. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the prediction about adaptor-density-dependent tuning and adds the notable implication that active energy consumption is not required, clarifying and extending the original predictive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remodeling and disassembly does not require ATPases, which is a critical advance towards predicting control of productive vesicle formation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model predicts how adaptor density can tune clathrin-coated structures from the transient to the stable, indicating that active energy consumption is not required for lattice disassembly or remodeling during growth. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert that active energy consumption (e.g., ATPases) is not required for disassembly/remodeling; the publication frames this within the adaptor-density tuning prediction.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The critical nucleus contains approximately 25 clathrin, which is remarkably similar to sizes of the transient and abortive structures observed in vivo. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The estimate of a ~25-clathrin critical nucleus appears as a new quantitative result in the publication that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The cost of bending the membrane could be largely offset by the energetic benefit of forming curved rather than flat structures. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The energetic balance comment about bending vs. curvature appears as an additional mechanistic/result statement in the publication not found in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Predicted contacts tend to form more bonds than non-predicted contacts, suggesting these contacts may be more important. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Predicted contacts tend to form more bonds than non-predicted contacts, suggesting these contacts may be more important than contacts that were not predicted. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same observation and interpretation with equivalent confidence and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetaPSICOV and DNCON2 favour accuracy whereas CCMpred detects contacts with more bonds. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetaPSICOV and DNCON2 favour accuracy, whereas CCMPred detects contacts with more bonds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core comparative finding is the same; the publication updates certainty but the content remains unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The push for higher accuracy in contact prediction may lead to a loss of physico-chemically important contacts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The push for higher accuracy may lead to a loss of physico-chemically important contacts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same cautionary conclusion about accuracy-driven losses of physico-chemical contacts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a connection between protein physico-chemistry and the coevolutionary couplings derived from multiple sequence alignments. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results underscore the connection between protein physico-chemistry and the coevolutionary couplings that can be derived from MSAs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same high-confidence conclusion linking physico-chemistry and coevolutionary couplings from MSAs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: miR-218-5p is identified as a key player in coordinating inhibitory and excitatory synapses during homeostatic synaptic plasticity and sleep. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: miR-218-5p is identified as a key player in coordinating inhibitory and excitatory synapses during homeostatic plasticity and sleep. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same high-confidence conclusion with equivalent scope and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inhibiting miR-218-5p activity prevents the downscaling of excitatory synapses and dendritic spines while blocking inhibitory synapse upscaling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Inhibiting miR-218-5p activity occludes the downscaling of excitatory synapses and dendritic spines while attenuating inhibitory synapse upscaling during homeostatic synaptic downscaling. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same effect on excitatory synapse downscaling but softens the inhibitory-synapse effect from 'blocking' to 'attenuating', a revision in strength of effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local inhibition of miR-218-5p in the somatosensory cortex reduces local slow-wave activity during non-rapid-eye-movement sleep. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning applications indicate necessary and sufficient parameters that predict arrhythmia vulnerability. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the machine-learning finding from a specific predictor (beat-to-beat instability) to a stronger statement about identifying necessary and sufficient predictive parameters, broadening and strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A prototype multiscale model framework was developed and validated to predict electro-toxicity in the heart for the proarrhythmic drug dofetilide. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multiscale model framework can predict electrotoxicity in the heart from the atom to the rhythm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution about the multiscale framework's ability to predict electrotoxicity; wording is generalized but the contribution matches.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model framework demonstrates feasibility for predicting cardiotoxicity using human clinical data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about feasibility using human clinical data that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Novel mechanistic insights into proarrhythmic drug interactions with the hERG channel were identified. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic insight about hERG interactions appears as a new conclusion in the publication that was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a novel approach for comparing GO evaluation metrics called Artificial Dilution Series (ADS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel approach for comparing GO evaluation metrics called Artificial Dilution Series (ADS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We compared 37 evaluation metrics for GO annotation using ADS and identified drastic differences between metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We compared 37 evaluation metrics for GO annotation using ADS and identified drastic differences between metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with unchanged certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some metrics struggle to differentiate between different signal levels, while others give erroneously high scores to the false positive data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some metrics struggle to differentiate between different signal levels, while others give erroneously high scores to the false positive data sets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication frames it as a conclusion with reduced stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide guidelines on which evaluation metrics perform well with the Gene Ontology and propose improvements to several well-known evaluation metrics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide guidelines on which evaluation metrics perform well with the Gene Ontology and propose improvements to several well-known evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same guideline/improvement claim but states it with higher certainty, increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADS is applicable to other areas of science where the evaluation of prediction results is non-trivial. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader applicability claim is present in the preprint but is not found in the publication text, so it appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical models tended to obtain better performance than first-principle methods in predicting binding affinities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical models tended to obtain better performance than first-principle methods in predicting binding affinities. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this comparative result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the methodologies generally displayed a substantial dependence on the system considered, emphasizing the need for host diversity in blind evaluations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the methodologies generally displayed a substantial dependence on the system considered, emphasizing the need for host diversity in blind evaluations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several entries that exploited previous experimental measurements succeeded in reducing systematic errors, but did not correspond to an improvement in statistical correlation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several entries that exploited previous experimental measurements succeeded in reducing systematic errors, but did not improve statistical correlation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly tightened but the meaning (reduced systematic error without improved correlation) is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There was an overall improvement in the correlation obtained by the affinity predictions for OA and TEMOA systems compared to previous rounds, but no improvement in root mean square error. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There has been an overall improvement in the correlation obtained by the affinity predictions for OA and TEMOA systems compared to previous rounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the correlation improvement but drops the explicit statement about no RMSE improvement and expresses the result with higher certainty, so the claim is revised and narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further refinement of force field parameters and improved treatment of chemical effects may be required to enhance predictive accuracy. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further refinement of force field parameters and improved treatment of chemical effects may be required to enhance predictive accuracy. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speculative recommendation is preserved verbatim with the same uncertainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel prediction algorithm based on a hidden Markov model allows for efficient training in peptide-spectrum matching. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our interpolated-HMM model, trained on millions of MS2 spectra, picks up meaningful patterns in peptide fragmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the HMM contribution more in terms of the model (interpolated-HMM) and its ability to learn patterns; the original emphasis on efficient training is not explicit, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall good reproducibility across different fragmentation spectra indicates a pattern to fragmentation that justifies using machine learning methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is high reproducibility across different fragmentation spectra given the precursor peptide and charge state, implying that there is a pattern to fragmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the reproducibility/pattern claim more strongly (higher stated certainty) while retaining the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model demonstrates a clear difference in prediction accuracy compared to a mock model, indicating it can learn meaningful patterns in the fragmentation process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our interpolated-HMM model, trained on millions of MS2 spectra, picks up meaningful patterns in peptide fragmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that the model learns meaningful fragmentation patterns; the publication presents this as a result of training on large data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model shows robust performance independent of the specific peptides present in the training data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performs well independent of the specific peptides that are present in the training data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the claim about peptide-independence directly and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model serves as a valuable pre-processing step in peptide identification by discerning unlikely intense fragment ions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The real value of this model is as a preprocessing step in the peptide identification process. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both texts identify preprocessing in peptide identification as the key practical use; the publication conveys the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The three selected hits from the virtual screenings of about 40 million compounds would possibly inhibit the enzyme, ATP sulfurylase, which is involved in the first reaction in hydrogen sulfide formation in Desulfobacter postgatei. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Consensus scoring using molecular docking and machine learning identified three potential inhibitors of ATP sulfurylase from a database of about 40 million compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the identification of three potential inhibitors more directly and with higher certainty, shifting from tentative language to a clearer identification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compound C is predicted to be both safe and effective as a potential inhibitor of ATP sulfurylase. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compound C was predicted to be both safe and effective as a potential inhibitor of ATP sulfurylase based on its physicochemical and toxicological properties. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the basis for the prediction (physicochemical and toxicological properties) but keeps the same conclusion and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The selected inhibitors are expected to significantly reduce the formation of hydrogen sulfide and consequently prevent the development of pyrite scale in oil and gas wells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the applied-impact claim about reducing H2S formation or preventing pyrite scale, so this prediction appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compound A had the highest docking score among the selected compounds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compound A had the highest docking score among the identified inhibitors. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The docking-score ranking is preserved, but the publication states this with reduced certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adaptive tree search heuristic implemented in RAxML-NG modifies the thoroughness of the tree search strategy based on the predicted difficulty of the dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On easy datasets, searches converge rapidly and can be terminated at an earlier stage, while overanalyzing difficult datasets is ineffective. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the idea as conclusions about dataset difficulty and early termination rather than the implementation detail; thus the methodological claim is reframed/clarified and stated with more cautious language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that the adaptive heuristic exhibits substantial speedups, especially on easy and difficult datasets, with average speedups of more than 10x. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adaptive tree search heuristic implemented in RAxML-NG demonstrates substantial speedups on datasets of varying difficulty, particularly achieving average speedups of more than 10× on easy and difficult datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance/speedup result verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximately 94% of the inferred trees using the adaptive strategy are statistically indistinguishable from the trees inferred under the standard strategy (RAxML-NG). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Approximately 94% of the inferred trees using the adaptive strategy are statistically indistinguishable from those inferred under the standard strategy (RAxML-NG). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative accuracy result exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Look4LTRs can annotate LTR-retrotransposons in multiple related genomes simultaneously and discover recently nested elements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Look4LTRs can annotate LTR-retrotransposons in multiple related genomes simultaneously and discover recently nested elements.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially identical in wording and meaning to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Look4LTRs is the fastest tool while maintaining better or comparable F1 scores to those obtained by other tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Look4LTRs is the fastest tool while maintaining better or comparable F1 scores to those obtained by other tools.
alignments.rationale: The performance and speed claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Expert human manual examination confirmed that three out of five recently nested LTR-retrotransposons were valid elements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Expert human manual examination revealed that three elements belong to known families and two elements are likely from new families.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more detail on the classification of the five elements (three known, two likely new) rather than simply stating confirmation of three, and uses somewhat less certain language for two elements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Look4LTRs represents a true advancement in the annotation of LTR-retrotransposons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Look4LTRs represents a true advancement in the annotation of LTR-retrotransposons, opening the door to many studies focused on understanding their functions in plants.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the advancement claim and expands it by emphasizing broader impacts on future studies; the publication expresses this with higher stated certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: LTR-retrotransposons are the most abundant type of transposons in plant genomes and have important functions involving gene regulation and the rise of new genes and pseudo genes in response to severe stress.
alignments.rationale: This background/biological-consequence statement appears in the publication but was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present the first evidence for vascular regulation driving fMRI signals in specific functional brain networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study presents the first evidence for vascular regulation driving fMRI signals in specific functional brain networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the novelty claim verbatim and with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three neuronal networks were identified as demonstrating maximum temporal correlation with the neuronal stimulus paradigms: Default Mode Network, Task Positive Network, and Visual Network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Three neuronal networks were identified as demonstrating maximum temporal correlation with the neuronal stimulus paradigms: Default Mode Network, Task Positive Network, and Visual Network.
alignments.rationale: The identified networks and their relation to neuronal stimulus paradigms are identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In each pairing, one network was dominated by the appropriate neuronal stimulus, and the other was dominated by the vascular stimulus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In each pairing of identified networks, one network was dominated by the appropriate neuronal stimulus, while the other was dominated by the vascular stimulus as represented by the end-tidal CO2 time-series.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result but clarifies how the vascular stimulus was represented (end-tidal CO2 time-series).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain’s vasculature may be regulated in a coordinated manner that mimics and potentially supports specific functional brain networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The brain's vasculature may be regulated in a coordinated manner that mimics and potentially supports specific functional brain networks.
alignments.rationale: The conceptual conclusion about coordinated vascular regulation is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding network-specific vascular function and the behavior of a coupled vascular-neural network will be critical in future studies of brain pathology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding network-specific vascular function and the behavior of a coupled vascular-neural network will be critical in future studies of brain pathology.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation/limitation for future work is restated unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Flysim platform successfully reconstructs a brain-wide network model of the Drosophila brain consisting of 20,089 neurons and 1,044,020 synapses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Flysim platform successfully reconstructs a brain-wide network model of the Drosophila brain consisting of 20,089 neurons and 1,044,020 synapses.
alignments.rationale: The reconstruction claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstructed Drosophila brain can be easily stabilized by implementing synaptic short-term depression, unlike the randomized brain network which exhibited seizure-like firing activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconstructed Drosophila brain can be easily stabilized by implementing synaptic short-term depression, unlike a randomized brain network which exhibited seizure-like firing activity.
alignments.rationale: The stabilization finding and contrast with a randomized network are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstructed Drosophila brain demonstrates greater structural and dynamic diversity compared to the randomized brain network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconstructed Drosophila brain is structurally and dynamically more diverse than the randomized brain network and exhibits both Poisson-like and patterned firing activities.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the diversity comparison and adds specific descriptions of firing activity types, expanding the detail of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This single-cell level brain model allows for the study of fundamental properties of neural networks, including network balance, critical behavior, long-term stability, and plasticity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This single-cell level brain model allows for the study of fundamental properties of neural networks including network balance, critical behavior, long-term stability, and plasticity.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about the model's utility is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can explain observed nucleation rates as well as the comparatively slow propagation speed of stage I retinal waves.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model presented can explain observed nucleation rates and the comparatively slow propagation speed of stage I retinal waves.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the model's explanatory ability for nucleation rates and slow propagation speed directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimated wave speed in the model network is derived from the interaction between two coupled neurons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated wave speed in the model network is derived from the interaction between two coupled neurons.
alignments.rationale: The origin of the estimated wave speed is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulations of small networks of neurons yield values of gap junction coupling and intrinsic noise level that are in a physiologically plausible range.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations of small networks of neurons yield values of gap junction coupling and intrinsic noise level that are in a physiologically plausible range.
alignments.rationale: The result is present in the publication but reported with slightly lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Waves emerge from rare stochastic fluctuations in single cells in the proposed model of gap-junction coupled noisy neurons.
alignments.rationale: The specific mechanistic statement about emergence from rare single-cell stochastic fluctuations does not appear explicitly in the publication's claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Spontaneous waves in the developing retina are essential for the formation of retinotopic mapping in the visual system.
alignments.rationale: This broader developmental-consequence claim appears in the publication but was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mouse visual system exhibits strong visually-mediated responses to flow stimuli that exceed predictions based on spatial frequency analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mouse visual system demonstrates strong visually mediated responses to flow stimuli that exceed predictions based on spatial-frequency analysis.
alignments.rationale: The core result about strong flow responses exceeding spatial-frequency predictions is stated similarly in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many neurons in the primary visual cortex (V1) of mice are orientation- or direction-selective and respond to higher spatial frequencies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many neurons in the primary visual cortex of mice exhibit orientation or direction selectivity in response to flow stimuli.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the selectivity claim specifically on responses to flow stimuli; the preprint also mentioned higher spatial frequencies which the publication addresses elsewhere.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conventional linear approaches to visual processing do not adequately capture the operation of the neural network in mouse V1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings challenge conventional linear approaches to visual processing in mice.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the challenge to linear models consistent with the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of visual flow patterns as stimuli expands our understanding of the mouse's visual capacity to behaviorally-relevant ranges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study expands the understanding of the mouse's visual capacity to behaviorally relevant ranges.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution claim about expanding understanding of visual capacity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Flow responses in the mouse visual system predominate in higher spatial-frequency ranges of 0.15-1.6 cycles per degree.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a more specific quantitative statement about the spatial-frequency range of flow responses that was not present as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical organoids exhibit phase-amplitude coupling between delta oscillation (2 Hz) and high-frequency activity (100-400 Hz) during network-synchronous events. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report phase-amplitude coupling details; no publication claim matches the specific PAC result, so this preprint claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Developmental impairment of MECP2-KO cortical organoids impacts the emergence of oscillatory activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention MECP2-KO or related developmental impairment results, so this specific experimental claim was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortical organoid network electrophysiological signatures correlate with human preterm neonatal EEG features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The oscillatory activity in human cortical organoids transitions to more spatiotemporally irregular patterns resembling features observed in preterm human electroencephalography. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the nature of the similarity (transitions to irregular patterns resembling preterm EEG), narrowing and clarifying the preprint's broader correlation claim and with somewhat lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of structured network activity in the human neocortex may follow stable genetic programming, even in the absence of external or subcortical inputs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of structured network activity in a human neocortex model may follow stable genetic programming. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about possible genetically driven development of structured network activity with essentially the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Functional neural circuitry in cortical organoids exhibits emergence and development of oscillatory network dynamics similar to those found in the developing human brain. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The oscillatory activity in human cortical organoids transitions to more spatiotemporally irregular patterns resembling features observed in preterm human electroencephalography. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames similarity more specifically as organoid oscillatory transitions that resemble preterm EEG features, narrowing the preprint's broader claim and expressing it with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heparan sulfates (HS) play a role in the formation and function of the male neural network in C. elegans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Heparan sulfates (HS) play a role in the formation and function of the male neural network in Caenorhabditis elegans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about HS in the C. elegans male neural network with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Loss of 3-O sulfation results in the presynaptic accumulation of RAB-3, disrupting the formation of synapses in the mating circuits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Loss of 3-O sulfation resulted in the presynaptic accumulation of RAB-3 and disrupted the formation of synapses in a component of the mating circuits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim mirrors the preprint result with only minor wording changes and the same reported effect and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural cell adhesion protein neurexin promotes and neural cell adhesion protein neuroligin inhibits the formation of the same set of synapses in a parallel pathway. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural cell adhesion protein NRX-1/neurexin promotes and the neural cell adhesion protein NLG-1/neuroligin inhibits the formation of the same set of synapses in a parallel pathway. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same antagonistic roles for neurexin and neuroligin, adding standardized gene names but not changing the claim's meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enzymatically-generated modifications of proteins, such as heparan sulfates, are crucial for the formation of specific synapses between neurons. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural cell adhesion proteins and extracellular matrix components act together in the formation of synaptic connections. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the conclusion to emphasize joint roles of adhesion proteins and ECM components rather than focusing specifically on enzymatic modifications, broadening the framing of the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decagon accurately predicts polypharmacy side effects, outperforming baselines by up to 69%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decagon accurately predicts polypharmacy side effects, outperforming baselines by up to 69%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decagon can predict the exact side effect, if any, through which a given drug combination manifests clinically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decagon can predict the exact side effect, if any, through which a given drug combination manifests clinically. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same specific predictive claim about exact side-effect identification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decagon automatically learns representations of side effects indicative of co-occurrence of polypharmacy in patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decagon automatically learns representations of side effects indicative of co-occurrence of polypharmacy in patients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The representational learning claim is unchanged in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decagon models particularly well polypharmacy side effects that have a strong molecular basis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decagon models particularly well polypharmacy side effects that have a strong molecular basis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement about modeling molecularly grounded side effects is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Decagon opens up opportunities to use large pharmacogenomic and patient population data to flag and prioritize polypharmacy side effects for follow-up analysis. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Decagon opens up opportunities to use large pharmacogenomic and patient population data to flag and prioritize polypharmacy side effects for follow-up analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same but the publication expresses greater confidence/importance (higher stated certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The zebrafish genome contains at least 23 GABA A subunits, with all but the mammalian θ and ε subunits having at least one zebrafish ortholog. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The zebrafish genome contains at least 23 GABAA subunits, with all but the mammalian θ and ε subunits having at least one zebrafish ortholog. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The genomic enumeration and orthology conclusion are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Zebrafish contain one GABA A subunit, β4, which does not have a clear mammalian ortholog. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Zebrafish contain one GABAA subunit, β4, which does not have a clear mammalian ortholog. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific subunit/orthology finding is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expression patterns of GABA A subunits in zebrafish are distinct across the brain, spinal cord, and retina during early development. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expression patterns of GABAA subunits in zebrafish demonstrate distinct patterns in the brain, spinal cord, and retina during early development. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The developmental expression pattern results are reported similarly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Subunit-specific expression patterns of GABA A receptors are similar between zebrafish, frogs, and rodents, suggesting conservation of isoform roles among vertebrates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Subunit-specific expression patterns in zebrafish resemble those found in frogs and rodents, suggesting that the roles of different GABAA receptor isoforms are largely conserved among vertebrates. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same comparative conclusion about conserved isoform roles across vertebrates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides a platform to examine isoform specific roles of GABA A receptors within zebrafish neural circuits. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides a platform to examine isoform specific roles of GABAA receptors within zebrafish neural circuits. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same but the publication states higher confidence/importance (certainty increased).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed headstage can successfully record 32 channels of raw and/or spike sorted data for over 24 hours. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed headstage can successfully record 32 channels of raw neural signal data and/or spike sorted events for over 24 hours. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core capability and measurement (32 channels, >24 hours) are stated consistently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system is robust to power dropouts during battery changes and SD card replacement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system demonstrates robustness to power dropouts during battery changes and SD card replacement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same robustness properties described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A 24-hour initial recording in a non-human primate M1 showed consistent spike shapes with expected changes in neural activity during awake behaviour and sleep cycles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports the specific non-human primate 24-hour recording details (spike-shape consistency and behavioral state changes), so this specific example appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented platform allows for unobtrusive monitoring and processing of neural activity in freely behaving untethered animals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform allows for real-time monitoring and processing of neural activity in freely behaving untethered animals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tightens the claim by specifying real-time monitoring and states higher certainty, amplifying the platform's capabilities relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system provides a robust, low-latency, low-bandwidth output suitable for BMIs, closed loop neuromodulation, wireless transmission, and long term data logging. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This system achieves the lowest power per channel to date, suitable for brain-machine interfaces and long-term data logging. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the system's suitability in terms of a specific metric (lowest power per channel) and increases stated confidence, making the claim more specific and stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Socioeconomic factors drive the reporting of tick-borne viruses at a country level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Wealthier nations with stronger institutional and professional capacity exhibit higher reporting rates of tick-borne virus cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the broad preprint statement by specifying wealth and institutional/professional capacity as the key socioeconomic drivers, narrowing the original generalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Countries with a lower Gini Index, increased spending on pesticide imports, and knowledgeable institutions or individuals are more likely to report tick-borne viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Countries with lower income inequality and greater expertise in veterinary, agricultural, or forestry sectors are more likely to report tick-borne virus cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the income-inequality and expertise elements but omits pesticide-import spending, shifting and narrowing the set of highlighted socioeconomic predictors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Countries with a lower percentage of the population exposed to conflict have a higher probability of reporting tick-borne viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Countries affected by conflict or limited health infrastructure show underreporting of tick-borne virus cases.
alignments.rationale: Both statements identify conflict-affected countries as exhibiting lower reporting; the publication frames it as underreporting, consistent with the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Broad environmental factors, such as climate classification, are important in identifying suitable zones for reported tick-borne viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Climatic factors, particularly subarctic environments, contribute to the likelihood of reporting tick-borne virus cases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the role of climate but specifies subarctic environments, narrowing and somewhat tempering the original broad environmental claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Socioeconomic interventions may be the most effective tool to reduce the local disease burden of tick-borne viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strengthening surveillance through targeted resource allocation and better integration of veterinary and public health expertise could enhance global tick-borne disease mitigation.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes surveillance and resource allocation rather than broad socioeconomic interventions to reduce burden, shifting and narrowing the intervention focus and tone.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cerebellar Purkinje cells encode changes in the position of the water source with complex spikes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unexpected changes in the target location trigger complex spikes that alter simple spike firing during subsequent licks, adjusting the tongue trajectory.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides mechanistic detail showing complex spikes are triggered by target-location changes and modulate subsequent licking, clarifying the preprint's encoding claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Purkinje cell simple spike firing affects the direction of tongue movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased simple spike firing during tongue protrusion induces ipsiversive bending of the tongue.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the directional effect (ipsiversive bending) associated with increased simple spike firing, directly clarifying the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using machine learning, we demonstrated that it is possible to predict licking activity based on the spiking patterns of individual Purkinje cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Purkinje cells can encode rhythmic licking with both their simple spikes and complex spikes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports encoding of licking by spikes but does not emphasize the machine-learning-based predictive demonstration claimed in the preprint, reducing the methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optogenetic stimulation of Purkinje cells suggests that increased simple spike activity causes ipsilateral bending of the tongue during goal-directed movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increased simple spike firing during tongue protrusion induces ipsiversive bending of the tongue.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same causal link with higher certainty and a clear description of ipsiversive bending, amplifying the original optogenetic conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Directional control of movements is paramount in cerebellar function and complex spike and simple spike modulation complement each other during sensorimotor coordination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modulation of the complex spikes and that of the simple spikes are complementary during acquisition and execution of sensorimotor coordination.
alignments.rationale: Both versions articulate that complex and simple spike modulation are complementary in sensorimotor coordination, with consistent scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Alterations in Purkinje cell activity accompany licking, with different Purkinje cells often exerting heterogeneous encoding schemes.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit claim about heterogeneity of encoding across Purkinje cells that was not clearly stated in the preprint and thus appears as an added observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Belowground biomass (BGB) of Spartina alterniflora varies more spatially than temporally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Belowground biomass (BGB) of Spartina alterniflora varies more spatially than temporally, as evidenced by field observations showing 69% of site comparisons differing compared to only 21% of year comparisons.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same result with additional quantitative field-evidence, maintaining the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Flooding frequency and intensity are the most influential variables in predicting BGB.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Flooding frequency and intensity are the most influential variables in predicting BGB, with hydrology-related predictor variables accounting for 61% of the total feature importance in the BERM framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms and quantifies the dominance of hydrology-related predictors in BGB prediction, consistent with the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advanced Belowground Ecosystem Resiliency Model (BERM) reduced model error from 13.0% to 9.4%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advanced BERM model reduced error from a normalized root-mean-square error of 13.0% to 9.4%, indicating improved predictive performance and expanded conditions for model application.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same improvement in predictive error and interprets it as enhanced model performance, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advanced BERM can characterize S. alterniflora productivity and resilience over broad spatial and temporal scales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model estimates from the advanced BERM reflect the spatiotemporal structure of BGB variation observed in field measurements.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the model claim more modestly in terms of reflecting observed spatiotemporal structure rather than broadly characterizing productivity and resilience across scales, narrowing and tempering the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-mer counting demonstrates a close correspondence with phylogenetically aware diversity metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results demonstrate a close correspondence with phylogenetically aware diversity metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result with consistent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-mer counting is a suitable and efficient strategy for feature processing prior to diversity estimation and supervised learning in microbiome surveys.
alignments.pub_claim_text: k-mer counting appears to be a suitable and efficient strategy for feature processing prior to diversity estimation and supervised learning in microbiome surveys.
alignments.rationale: The publication's language and claim align directly with the preprint's conclusion about k-mer counting as a feature-processing strategy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-mer-based diversity estimates are useful proxy measurements when reliable phylogenies are not available.
alignments.pub_claim_text: k-mer-based diversity estimates are useful proxy measurements especially when reliable phylogenies are not available.
alignments.rationale: Both statements make the same qualification about k-mer-based diversity estimates serving as proxies in the absence of reliable phylogenies.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: K-mer frequencies from marker-gene sequence surveys can inform diversity estimates and machine learning predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work demonstrates that k-mer frequencies from marker-gene sequence surveys can be used to inform diversity estimates and machine learning predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result about k-mer frequencies informing diversity estimates and predictive models without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synapse proteins are identified as the strongest correlates of cognitive impairment in Alzheimer's disease, independent of amyloid-beta and tau.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synapse proteins emerged as the strongest correlates of cognitive impairment in Alzheimer's disease, independent of amyloid-beta and tau.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result directly, preserving its meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CSF YWHAG:NPTX2 synapse protein ratio explains 27% of the variance in cognitive impairment beyond CSF PTau181:Aβ42.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CSF YWHAG:NPTX2 synapse protein ratio explained 27% of the variance in cognitive impairment beyond CSF pTau181:Aβ42.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative explanatory power for the CSF ratio, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Individuals in the top 25th percentile of YWHAG:NPTX2 are 15 times more likely to experience cognitive decline over 15 years compared to those in the bottom 25th percentile.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CSF YWHAG:NPTX2 predicted conversion from cognitively normal to mild cognitive impairment with a hazard ratio of 3.0.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different, more modest hazard ratio and a specific conversion endpoint (to MCI), reducing the magnitude and altering the original long-term quartile comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that CSF YWHAG:NPTX2 and the corresponding plasma signature are robust prognostic biomarkers for Alzheimer's disease onset and progression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings underscore CSF YWHAG:NPTX2 as a robust prognostic biomarker for cognitive resilience versus Alzheimer's disease onset and progression.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the CSF ratio as a robust prognostic biomarker, clarifying the focus on CSF measures though the core conclusion about prognostic value remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YWHAG:NPTX2 levels increase with normal aging and at a faster rate in APOE4 carriers and autosomal dominant-AD mutation carriers.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate the aging- or genotype-related trajectory claim about YWHAG:NPTX2, indicating this preprint claim was not carried into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: True zero-shot cross-user myoelectric control is achievable without user-specific training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: True zero-shot cross-user myoelectric control is achievable without user-specific training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the exact conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A classification accuracy of 93.0% for six gestures was achieved on a set of 306 unseen users. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A classification accuracy of 93.0% for six gestures was achieved on a set of 306 unseen users. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and wording are identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Big data approaches can enable robust cross-user myoelectric control compared to most EMG studies that typically employ only 10-20 users. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large data models can effectively generalize to new datasets and mitigate the impact of common confounding factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the idea on generalization and mitigation of confounders and drops the explicit comparison to small-sample EMG studies; wording is stronger/certain in the pub version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The large data models can effectively generalize to new datasets and mitigate the impact of common confounding factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large data models can effectively generalize to new datasets and mitigate the impact of common confounding factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This claim is essentially reproduced verbatim in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MJF-14 PLA demonstrates unprecedented specificity for detecting aggregated α-synuclein. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MJF-14 PLA demonstrates unprecedented specificity for detecting aggregated α-synuclein. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the central claim of high specificity; wording and certainty are effectively the same, only the claim type label changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Signal in the MJF-14 PLA directly correlates with α-synuclein aggregation in SH-SY5Y cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Signal in the MJF-14 PLA correlates with α-synuclein aggregation in cell culture and human neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the experimental systems (adds human neurons) beyond the SH-SY5Y cell line, clarifying and broadening the evidence base while maintaining high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MJF-14 PLA reveals extensive non-inclusion pathology in post-mortem dementia with Lewy bodies cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MJF-14 PLA reveals extensive non-inclusion pathology in post-mortem cases of dementia with Lewy bodies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The substance of the claim is preserved but the publication reports it with higher certainty, indicating strengthened evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Striking α-synuclein pathology is observed preceding the formation of conventional Lewy pathology in Parkinson’s disease brain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Widespread α-synuclein pathology is observed in Parkinson’s disease brain prior to the formation of conventional Lewy pathology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the observation as 'widespread' and retains high certainty, expanding the descriptive scope of the pathology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the non-inclusion pathology revealed by the PLA is located in the neuropil, including presynaptic terminals. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or restate the anatomical localization to neuropil/presynaptic terminals, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean activity of different cortical regions could be decoded from locally recorded surface potentials. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean activity of different cortical regions could be decoded from locally recorded surface potentials. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core decoding result is restated identically in the publication with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Each frequency band of surface potentials differentially encodes activities from multiple cortical regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Each frequency band of surface potentials differentially encodes activities from multiple cortical regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same finding about frequency-band specific encoding without substantive change.
