alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work strongly suggests the existence of rotor rings with previously undescribed high stoichiometry in natural organisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work strongly suggests the existence of rotor rings with previously undescribed high stoichiometry in natural organisms.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the tentative conclusion about previously undescribed high-stoichiometry rotor rings with similar hedging.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the utility of AlphaFold-based approaches for studying homooligomeric proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high-throughput computational approach based on AlphaFold can estimate the stoichiometry of all homo-oligomeric c-rings present in genomic databases.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the concrete capability (stoichiometry estimation) of the AlphaFold-based pipeline rather than the broader, more general statement about utility, narrowing the original conceptual claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The most stable combinatorial variant exhibited a denaturation temperature exceeding 89�C, representing a greater than 17�C improvement over wild-type hMb.
alignments.rationale: The reported denaturation temperature and improvement are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Surface display levels serve as a proxy for thermostability of soluble hMb variants, showing strong correlations with machine learning stability prediction algorithms.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings demonstrate the capabilities of DMS-assisted combinatorial protein engineering to guide the discovery of thermostable variants.

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the SN coupling/decoupling statement but omits the ESN synchronization with FPN and DMN present in the preprint, narrowing the scope of the original paired claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The degree of decoupling between the SN and DMN was associated with semantic performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The degree of decoupling between the left-lateralized semantic network and the default mode network was associated with semantic task performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same association with slightly more specific network labeling (left-lateralized SN) but preserves the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Human higher cognition is achieved by the neural dynamics of brain networks, serving distinct and shared cognitive functions depending on task demands. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Human higher cognition is achieved by multiple brain networks that serve distinct and shared cognitive functions depending on task demands. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding claim with minor wording changes that do not alter meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ExpressionGAN can generate de novo functional regulatory DNA with prespecified expression levels by modeling whole gene regulatory structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ExpressionGAN can traverse the entire regulatory sequence-expression landscape in a gene-specific manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's generative claim as traversal of the sequence-expression landscape in a gene-specific way, a close restatement that clarifies the mechanism without changing the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generative strategy using ExpressionGAN is more efficient than a mutational approach when utilizing purely natural genomic data.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about superiority to a mutational approach using purely natural data appears in the publication, so the specific efficiency comparison was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 57% of the newly-generated highly-expressed sequences surpass the expression levels of natural controls.
alignments.pub_claim_text: 57% of the highly-expressed synthetic sequences surpass the expression levels of highly-expressed natural controls.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numeric performance statement with the same meaning and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for the production of sequence variants with target mRNA levels and natural-like properties, including over 30% dissimilarity to any natural sequence.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific claim about target mRNA levels combined with the stated >30% dissimilarity statistic, so this specific claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural correlates of coherence are similar across speaking and listening, particularly in default mode regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural correlates of coherence are similar across speaking and listening, particularly in default-mode regions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this finding verbatim (minor hyphenation change only), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Greater activation in left inferior prefrontal cortex is associated with more coherent discourse production.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Greater activation in left inferior prefrontal cortex is associated with more coherent discourse production.
alignments.rationale: This region-specific association is carried through identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semantic control network plays different roles in comprehension and production of discourse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The semantic control network plays different roles in comprehension and production of discourse.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about differential roles of the semantic control network is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Activation in default mode regions increases when less coherent speech is heard or produced.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Activation in default-mode regions reflects updating of mental representations when discourse does not conform to the expected topic.
alignments.rationale: The publication interprets the same activation as reflecting updating of mental representations when discourse is unexpected, providing a mechanistic clarification of the preprint's observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Satelight model, which uses the self-attention mechanism, detected epileptic spikes more effectively than other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Satelight model detected epileptic spikes more effectively than other models, achieving an accuracy of 0.876 and a false positive rate of 0.133.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the detection superiority claim and embeds it in the quantitative performance statement, keeping the core claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved an accuracy of 0.876 and a false positive rate of 0.133.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Satelight model detected epileptic spikes more effectively than other models, achieving an accuracy of 0.876 and a false positive rate of 0.133.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the exact performance metrics reported in the preprint; the metric claim is reproduced verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model had only one-tenth the number of parameters as other effective models while maintaining high detection performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Satelight model had only one-tenth the number of parameters compared to other effective models while maintaining high detection performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the parameter-efficiency claim directly, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model automatically attended to the EEG’s characteristic waveform locations of interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-attention mechanism in the Satelight model is expected to reduce the number of parameters and efficiently extract features from a small amount of EEG data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's specific observation about attending to waveform locations is softened in the publication to an expected/interpretative statement about efficient feature extraction and parameter reduction, reducing the specificity and confidence of the mechanistic claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nonlinear transient amplification (NTA) reconciles symmetric connectivity with rapid amplification while avoiding issues associated with Hebbian amplification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonlinear transient amplification (NTA) reconciles strong recurrent excitation with rapid amplification while avoiding issues associated with Hebbian amplification.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same high-level conclusion though the wording shifts from 'symmetric connectivity' to 'strong recurrent excitation'; the core reconciliation claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NTA has two distinct temporal phases: initial positive feedback excitation followed by short-term plasticity that stabilizes the network state.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NTA has two distinct temporal phases: an initial phase of positive feedback excitation and a subsequent phase where short-term plasticity stabilizes the network state.
alignments.rationale: The two-phase temporal description is restated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The onset transients produced by NTA are stimulus selective and well-suited for speedy information processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The onset transients resulting from NTA are stimulus selective and well-suited for speedy information processing.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this result with equivalent wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Excitatory-inhibitory co-tuning widens the parameter regime in which NTA is possible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Excitatory-inhibitory co-tuning widens the parameter regime in which NTA is possible in the absence of persistent activity.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'in the absence of persistent activity,' narrowing the conditions under which the preprint's claim holds and thus clarifying the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A-MUD outperformed the other detection methods in terms of true positive rates for recordings with low or high signal-to-noise ratios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A-MUD and USVSEG outperformed the other methods in terms of true positive rates using default and adjusted settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication modifies the performance landscape by indicating that USVSEG also outperformed other methods, so the preprint's implication that A-MUD uniquely outperformed others is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All four automated detection methods had comparable rates of detection failure.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports differences between methods when false detection rates are considered (e.g., A-MUD outperforming USVSEG under some conditions), so the blanket claim of comparable failure rates is not retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method BootSnap successfully classified calls into 12 types, including a new class of false positives used for detection refinement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BootSnap successfully classified calls into 12 types, including a new class of false positives that is useful for detection refinement.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the classification result and the introduction of a false-positive class for refinement, preserving the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BootSnap provides enhanced performance compared to state-of-the-art tools and has improved generalizability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BootSnap outperformed the pretrained and retrained state-of-the-art tool, and thus it is more generalizable.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which state-of-the-art tool was outperformed (pretrained and retrained), clarifying and making the generalizability claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA class I and class II differential expression outperformed traditional clinical metrics in predicting patient survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLA gene expression data can be used to predict the immune subtypes of patients receiving immune checkpoint blockade and stratify patient survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the predictive value of HLA expression for survival but removes the explicit claim of outperforming traditional clinical metrics and focuses on stratification in the context of immune checkpoint blockade.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patients with high HLA allelic diversity and gene expression had better prognosis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLA II genes were upregulated in an inflammatory immune subtype associated with best prognosis and were systematically downregulated in specific oncogenic pathways. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication links HLA II expression (not allelic diversity) to favorable prognosis in a specific inflammatory subtype, narrowing and altering the original claim about allelic diversity plus expression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Immune microenvironments with varied survival outcomes could be predicted using a neural network model trained on HLA expression data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HLA gene expression data can be used to predict the immune subtypes of patients receiving immune checkpoint blockade and stratify patient survival. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the predictive role of HLA expression but specifies patient context (ICB recipients) and survival stratification, and reports the result with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A subset of tumors upregulated HLA class I but not class II genes and exploited HLA-mediated escape strategies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher tumor immunogenicity mediated by HLA expression may sometimes lead to tumor escape under strong selective pressure. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames HLA-mediated escape as a possible outcome under selection pressure rather than asserting a subset of tumors actively upregulated class I but not class II to exploit escape, softening and narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HLA upregulation plays a crucial role in shaping prolonged anti-tumor immunity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not support the general conclusion that HLA upregulation drives prolonged anti-tumor immunity; instead it emphasizes predictive value and potential for escape, so this broader conclusion is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BaseLess can accurately determine the identity of reads between three closely related fish species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BaseLess can accurately determine the identity of reads between three closely related fish species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim and with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BaseLess can classify sequences in mixtures of twenty bacterial species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BaseLess can classify sequences in mixtures of 20 bacterial species on an inexpensive single-board computer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the classification capability but adds the operational detail that this can be done on a cheap single-board computer, clarifying deployment context.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BaseLess enables target-detection-only analysis by directly detecting the target sequence in the electrical signal. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational cost of analysis may be reduced by directly detecting the target sequence in the electrical signal. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication couches the idea more cautiously (a potential reduction in computational cost) rather than asserting a concrete 'target-detection-only analysis' capability, weakening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BaseLess operates effectively on an inexpensive single-board computer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BaseLess can classify sequences in mixtures of 20 bacterial species on an inexpensive single-board computer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the single-board computer performance, matching the preprint claim without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BaseLess utilizes an array of small neural networks to efficiently detect a fixed-size subsequence of the target sequence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific architectural detail (array of small neural networks) that was not present in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NMDA receptor activation is essential for the formation of long-term memory traces. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study confirms that NMDA receptor activation is essential for the formation of long-term memory traces. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly restates the original conclusion with the same strength and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In APV-treated cultures, short-term memory of stimuli persisted and they were still able to predict. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In APV-treated cultures, short-term memory of stimuli persisted and they were still able to predict. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental observation is repeated verbatim in the publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In contrast to control cultures, prediction remained fully dependent on short-term memory in APV-treated cultures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Prediction remained fully dependent on short-term memory in APV-treated cultures, in contrast to control cultures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative experimental result exactly and with the same confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: As control cultures learn to memorize the stimulus, long-term memory starts to contribute to their predictive capability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NMDA receptor activation plays a crucial role in enabling long-term memory. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same mechanistic conclusion about long-term memory contribution in slightly more general terms; the core idea is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel CEL model mitigates the catastrophic forgetting phenomenon in a domain incremental setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CEL model mitigates the catastrophic forgetting phenomenon in a domain incremental setting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution and result with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CEL outperforms other state-of-the-art models in several contexts, as indicated by high R-squared values during evaluation and reevaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CEL outperforms other state-of-the-art models in several contexts based on high R-squared values during evaluation and reevaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is repeated in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CEL demonstrates a minimal 65% forgetting rate and 18% higher memory stability compared to existing benchmark studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CEL demonstrates a minimal 65% forgetting rate and 18% higher memory stability compared to existing benchmark studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance metrics are carried over exactly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CEL's versatility in disease outbreak prediction addresses evolving data with temporal patterns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights CEL's versatility in disease outbreak prediction by addressing evolving data with temporal patterns. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claimed application versatility for outbreak prediction with comparable emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons, especially those beyond early sensory areas, act as optimal feedback controllers that steer their environment towards a specific desired state through their output. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons are conceptualized as optimal feedback controllers that steer their environment toward a specific desired state through their output. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conceptual conclusion essentially unchanged in scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Direct Data-Driven Control (DD-DC) framework models neurons as biologically feasible controllers that can identify loop dynamics, infer latent states, and optimize control. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The direct data-driven control (DD-DC) framework is utilized to model neurons as biologically feasible controllers that optimize control and infer latent states. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same framework contribution with equivalent claims about capabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DD-DC neuron model explains various neurophysiological phenomena, including the shift from potentiation to depression in Spike-Timing-Dependent Plasticity (STDP) and the imprecision in spike generation under constant stimulation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DD-DC neuron model explains various neurophysiological phenomena, including the shift from potentiation to depression in spike-timing-dependent plasticity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly documents the STDP shift claim (with higher certainty) but splits/imparts other phenomena (e.g., spike imprecision) into a separate claim, clarifying the specific phenomena supported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model presents a significant departure from the traditional McCulloch-Pitts-Rosenblatt neuron, offering a novel and biologically-informed fundamental unit for constructing neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model represents a significant departure from traditional neuron models, offering a modern, biologically informed fundamental unit for constructing neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim about the model's novelty and significance without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accounts for the imprecision in spike generation under constant stimulation and the operational variability and noise in the brain. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates and states the spike imprecision and variability claims explicitly; this specific phrasing/claim was not present in the preprint list and is thus treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetalPrognosis predicts disease-associated mutations within metal-binding sites of metalloproteins with heightened precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetalPrognosis predicts disease-associated mutations within metal-binding sites of metalloproteins with heightened precision. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication sentences are effectively identical in content and confidence, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MetalPrognosis outperforms leading methodologies like MCCNN and PolyPhen-2 across various metalloprotein test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MetalPrognosis outperforms leading methodologies like MCCNN and M-Ionic across various metalloprotein test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core claim of outperforming competitors is retained but the specific comparator changed (PolyPhen-2 -> M-Ionic), so the claim was revised and slightly narrowed in scope of named baselines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model architecture of MetalPrognosis has been validated through an ablation study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model architecture of MetalPrognosis has been shown to be effective through an ablation study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording shifted from 'validated' to 'shown to be effective' but the substantive result (ablation study supports architecture) is the same, so it is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Identifying disease-associated mutation sites within metal-binding regions is crucial for understanding protein function and drug development. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader concluding statement appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication claims, so it was removed from the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method outperformed graph-Fourier and convolutional neural-network-based methods in predicting the biophysical properties of proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method outperformed graph-Fourier and convolutional neural-network-based methods in predicting the biophysical properties of proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is identical in content and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method can summarize the effect of an amino acid based on its location and neighbourhood in protein-structure using graph-wavelet to estimate its influence on the biophysical property of proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can summarize the effect of an amino acid based on its location and neighbourhood in protein-structure using graph-wavelet to estimate its influence on the biophysical property of proteins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication reports lower certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered while semantics/scope remain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimate of the influence of amino-acid has the potential to explain the mechanism of the effect of deleterious non-synonymous mutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimate of the influence of amino-acid has the potential to explain the mechanism of the effect of deleterious non-synonymous mutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can reveal patterns of distribution of amino-acid properties in the structure of the protein in the context of a biophysical property for better classification and more insightful understanding. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach can reveal patterns of distribution of amino-acid properties in the structure of the protein in the context of a biophysical property for better classification and more insightful understanding. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is retained verbatim with same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A significant proportion of initially random model network nodes developed into divergent hubs within the characteristic complex network architecture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A significant proportion of initially random model network nodes developed into divergent hubs within the characteristic complex network architecture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proportion of divergent hubs depends parametrically on the wave incidence rate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proportion of nodes that developed into divergent hubs depends parametrically on the wave incidence rate. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same parametric dependence claim with equivalent wording; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher wave incidence rates increase the likelihood of hub formation while increasing the potential of ganglion cell death. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher wave incidence rates increase the likelihood of hub formation while increasing the potential for ganglion cell death. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal waves control the formation of convergence in the lateral geniculate nucleus (LGN). (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retinal waves control the formation of convergence in the lateral geniculate nuclei. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive conclusion is the same, but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Retinal waves stochastically control the distribution of converging and diverging activity in evolving complex networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Retinal waves stochastically control the distribution of converging and diverging activity in evolving complex networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim and certainty are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced stimulus representation in the frontal cortex tracks demands for active working memory control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhanced stimulus representation in the frontal cortex tracks demands for active working memory control. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhanced stimulus representation in the visual cortex tracks demands for precise working memory maintenance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhanced stimulus representation in the visual cortex tracks demands for precise working memory maintenance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differential frontal stimulus representation trades off with newly-generated category representation under varying control demands. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differential frontal stimulus representation trades off with newly-generated category representation under varying control demands. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide empirical and computational evidence that flexible stimulus representation in the frontal cortex serves as a potential neural coding scheme for working memory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide empirical and computational evidence that flexible stimulus representation in the frontal cortex serves as a potential neural coding scheme for working memory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion and its framing are unchanged in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rastermap is a visualization framework developed to help find patterns in neural data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rastermap is a visualization method that displays neurons as a raster plot based on their activity patterns. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same tool for visualizing neural activity; the publication uses more specific wording about display format, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rastermap utilizes a new global optimization algorithm for sorting neural responses along a one-dimensional manifold. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about a novel global optimization algorithm does not appear in the publication claims, indicating the detail was removed or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rastermap has been benchmarked on realistic simulations with multiplexed cognitive variables. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rastermap was benchmarked on realistic simulations and successfully used to explore recordings of tens of thousands of neurons from mouse cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The benchmarking on realistic simulations is explicitly retained; the publication additionally reports large-scale usage, but the core benchmarking claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rastermap has been demonstrated on recordings from various neural systems, including mouse visual and sensorimotor cortex, whole-brain zebrafish recordings, and rat hippocampus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rastermap was applied to various types of neural recordings, including whole-brain zebrafish recordings and electrophysiological recordings in multiple species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe application to multiple recording types/species; the publication rephrases and slightly reorganizes examples, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rastermap and similar algorithms cannot be used effectively in high-dimensional scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rastermap and similar algorithms cannot be used effectively in high-dimensional scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CREaTor can model cell type-specific cis-regulatory patterns in new cell types without prior knowledge of cCRE-gene interactions or additional training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CREaTor can model cell type-specific cis-regulatory patterns in new cell types without prior knowledge of cCRE-gene interactions or additional training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim verbatim about zero-shot modeling for cell type-specific cis-regulatory patterns, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CREaTor outperforms existing methods in capturing cCRE-gene interactions across various distance ranges in held-out cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report an explicit performance-vs-existing-methods claim; related publication text about model design/scale does not assert the specific outperforming claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The superior performance of CREaTor can be attributed to its capacity to model regulatory interactions at multiple levels, including higher-order genome organizations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not attribute performance to modeling higher-order genome organization; it emphasizes model design and generalization but omits this mechanistic explanation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CREaTor is a powerful tool for systematically investigating cis-regulatory programs across various cell types, both in normal developmental processes and disease-associated contexts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The zero-shot modeling capability of CREaTor allows for the ready generalization to a broad range of cell types. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes zero-shot generalization to many cell types but drops the explicit application framing to development and disease and tones the certainty to medium, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A recurrent neural network (RNN) can accurately automate the classification of firefly flash patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A recurrent neural network (RNN) can accurately automate firefly flash pattern classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core claim about RNN-based automated classification with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated classification method achieves a precision and recall of approximately seventy percent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated classification method achieves approximately seventy percent accuracy in classifying firefly species. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports ~70% as overall accuracy rather than the preprint's precision/recall metrics, representing a revision in the performance metric while retaining similar magnitude.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automated classification enables the study of firefly behavior at the population level. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
